title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduction to intelligent systems, search, knowledge representation, reasoning, planning, and machine learning foundations.
prerequisites: Graduate standing or approval of instructor.
topics: search algorithms, knowledge graphs, reasoning, planning, ML

title: Machine Learning
description: Supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, neural networks, probabilistic learning, and optimization methods.
prerequisites: Linear algebra, probability, and programming experience.
topics: classification, regression, clustering, neural networks, optimization

title: Deep Learning
description: Deep neural networks for representation learning with applications in vision, natural language processing, and sequence modeling.
topics: CNNs, transformers, RNNs, optimization, computer vision, NLP

title: Computer Vision
description: Image formation, feature extraction, object recognition, image segmentation, motion analysis, and visual learning.
topics: image processing, recognition, segmentation, visual learning

title: Distributed Systems
description: Distributed architectures, fault tolerance, consensus, replication, scalability, and cloud-based service design.
topics: consensus, replication, cloud systems, distributed computing

title: Information Security
description: Security principles, cryptography basics, system attacks, access control, secure protocols, and risk analysis.
topics: cybersecurity, cryptography, access control, secure systems

title: Data Mining and Analysis
description: Methods for extracting patterns from large datasets, including clustering, association analysis, anomaly detection, and predictive analytics.
topics: data mining, anomaly detection, pattern discovery, analytics

title: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Specification and implementation of abstract data types and associated algorithms including lists, stacks, queues, sorting, searching, graphs, and hashing.
topics: data structures, algorithms, graphs, sorting, analysis

title: Introduction to Computer Systems
description: Computer system organization, machine-level programming, memory hierarchy, concurrency basics, and systems programming concepts.

title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Fundamental concepts and techniques of intelligent systems including knowledge representation, search, planning, intelligent agents, and applications.

title: Machine Learning
description: Foundations of machine learning, pattern recognition, supervised and unsupervised learning, predictive models, clustering, and deep learning.
topics: machine learning, pattern recognition, clustering, deep learning

title: Introduction to Digital Systems Design
description: Design of digital logic systems, combinational and sequential circuits, hardware description methods, and system implementation.
topics: digital logic, circuits, hardware design, sequential systems

title: Computer Architecture and Design
description: Instruction set architecture, processor design, pipelining, memory hierarchy, and hardware-software interaction.
prerequisites: Digital systems design and programming background.
topics: computer architecture, processors, pipelining, memory systems

title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Core machine learning methods including supervised learning, neural networks, feature engineering, model evaluation, and engineering applications.
topics: machine learning, neural networks, feature engineering, prediction

title: Special Topics in Machine Learning Systems
description: Advanced topics in large-scale machine learning systems, hardware-aware optimization, acceleration, and deployment of intelligent systems.
prerequisites: Graduate standing and background in machine learning or computer architecture.
topics: machine learning systems, acceleration, optimization, deployment

title: Advanced Computer Architecture
description: Advanced processor architecture, parallel systems, memory hierarchy, accelerators, and performance optimization for modern computing platforms.
prerequisites: Computer architecture background and graduate standing.
topics: parallel systems, accelerators, processors, performance, architecture
